title: Binder × Are.na: modernize into an Are.na-driven static site
description: ## Notes **Domain.** Binder is a self-hostable static single-page site: it renders a navigable menu and loads whichever entry you pick into a full-viewport `

title: Research the Are.na public API: channels, blocks, embedding & CORS
description: ## Question What does the Are.na **public** REST API expose for reading a channel, and can a browser use it directly? Establish the facts the rest of the map depends on: - Endpoints to read a public channel and its contents (`GET /channels/:slug`, `/channels/:slug/contents`), pagination (`page` / `per`), and rate limits. - The **block types** (Link, Image, Text, Media/embed, Attachment, Channel-connection, …) and, for each, the fields that yield a URL / embed HTML / thumbnail. - **CORS:** does the API send headers allowing browser-side `fetch` from an arbitrary static origin, with no proxy? (Load-bearing for the runtime-fetch decision.) - How **nested channels** and **connections** are represented (the raw material for the "richer organizing layer"). Deliver a markdown summary as a linked asset. Use `/research`. ## Answer Full write-up: **`docs/research/arena-public-api.md`** (verified live against `api.are.na/v2` on 2026-07-11 + V2 docs). - **Runtime browser fetch works, no proxy.** Public *and* "closed" channels read with **no auth**; the API returns **`Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`** on GET and OPTIONS preflight. Green-lights the runtime-fetch decision. Only *private* channels need a token (OAuth code/token flow — unsafe for a static site; confirms public-only). - **Endpoints:** primary read is `GET /channels/:slug?per=100&page=K` (returns channel meta + `length` + a page of `contents`). `/contents` is lighter but omits `length`. Nested channels via `GET /channels/:slug/connections`. **`per` caps at 100** → fetch-all = `ceil(length/100)` pages. - **Six block classes**, fields verified: **Link** (`source.url` → external site, iframe target), **Media** (`embed.html` = embedly iframe → framing-safe for YouTube/Vimeo/Bandcamp), **Image** (`image.*.url` → ``), **Attachment** (`attachment.url` + `content_type`, e.g. PDF), **Text** (`content_html` inline), **Channel** (nested connection: `slug`/`title`/`length`). `generated_title` is a ready nav label; filter `state == "Available"`. - **Framing constraint confirmed:** Link blocks hit arbitrary sites, many of which refuse iframes (`X-Frame-Options`/CSP — NYT/Twitter/FB/GitHub per the README). Media dodges it via embedly. → feeds `binder-ab29`. - **Risks:** V2 is **deprecated** (V3 docs login-gated, unverified) → new ticket to evaluate V3; and **no rate-limit headers** are exposed → be a courteous runtime client (feeds the "runtime resilience" fog). Cascade: closing this unblocks `binder-7ac4` (organizing model) and `binder-ab29` (embedding).
closeReason: V2 public API: no-auth + CORS * (runtime fetch works, no proxy); 6 block classes mapped; nested channels via /connections; per caps at 100. Risks: V2 deprecated, no rate-limit headers. Asset: docs/research/arena-public-api.md

title: Choose the framework and build setup
description:
## Question

Which light framework + build setup should the modernized Binder use? It is a small, runtime-fetch, static-output app — essentially an iframe host with a dynamic (and, per the organizing-layer decision, likely interactive / nested) navigation.

Compare candidates — Svelte, Preact, Astro, SolidJS, Lit — against: runtime size, quality of **static** output, DX / build simplicity (Vite), and fit for a reactive nested nav. Recommend one with rationale.

Note the interaction with *Design the channel-to-navigation organizing model* (a richer nav may favor a reactive framework), but this is decidable now on general criteria. Use `/grilling`.

## Answer

Decision: **Svelte 5 (runes) + Vite**, static SPA output. Full writeup: **`docs/design/framework.md`**.

- **Chosen by the maintainer** for authoring ergonomics + reactivity/scoped-styling fit (9/9 on reactivity and styling for Binder's nested nav + signature panel).
- **Honest tradeoff:** a blind 5-way eval (scored /60) ranked **Preact 53, Solid 51, Lit 51, Svelte 49, Astro 37** — Preact edged it on size (Svelte's ~10 kB gzip runes floor vs ~6 kB) and contributor pool. The ~4 kB gzip delta is negligible for a self-hosted static app, and the judge endorsed "if the team lives in Svelte, take Svelte."
- **Framework-neutral:** the drag = native Pointer Events, hash-routing = native `onhashchange`, DOMPurify = a shared ~10 kB cost — none of Binder's distinctive features hinge on the pick.
- **Gotchas to design around:** scoped styles don't reach `{@html}` (style Text/Media renderers via `:global()`); use `SvelteMap` for the channel cache; hand-roll hash routing (not SvelteKit); the skeleton box covers client-render first paint.
- **Build:** `@sveltejs/vite-plugin-svelte`; `vite build` → static `dist/`; drop jQuery/jQuery-UI/touch-punch/modernizr.

Unblocks the codebase-migration and deployment fog (migration also waits on config, `binder-5299`). No new ticket.
closeReason: Decided: Svelte 5 (runes) + Vite, static SPA. Chosen for reactivity/styling fit + ergonomics; blind 5-way eval edged Preact on size (53 vs 49) but delta negligible self-hosted. Gotchas captured. docs/design/framework.md

title: Design the channel-to-navigation organizing model
description: ## Question Design how an Are.na channel's structure maps to Binder's navigation and embedding — the "richer organizing layer." Given the real API shape (see *Research the Are.na public API*): - What is a nav entry — a block? a channel? a connection? - How do **nested channels** and **connections** surface — nested menu, sections, a graph? - How do different **block types** behave when selected — iframe, embed, inline? - What happens to the current signature UX — the draggable movable menu, the full-viewport iframe, the `.about` text? Make it concrete with a `/prototype` (outline or stub UI) to react to. Blocked until the API facts land. ## Answer Decided model: **`docs/design/organizing-model.md`**. Settled via grilling (4 forks) + an adversarial critic pass that caught real gaps, all folded in. - **Root = SEVERAL top-level channels ("sections").** Each section = a channel of ordered `available` blocks + nested `Channel` drill-nodes + a connections strip; the root is a *synthetic* channel, so the whole tree is uniform. - **A nav entry** = a block (by `position`, `generated_title` label, `class` tag). `Channel` blocks (in `contents`) = drill nodes; `/connections` (items carry `slug`) = sideways jumps. - **Block behavior** = a per-class renderer in one full-viewport slot: Link→iframe *(framing-safe only; fallback → `binder-ab29`)*, Media→`embed.html`, Image→``, Text→`content_html`, Attachment→PDF. - **Signature UX preserved:** draggable box-shadow panel + full-viewport slot; logo/about **folded into the panel header**; active row **black**. - **Hardened after critique:** keep **hash-routing** (deep-link by stable block **id**, not ordinal; landing defers to the hash); a **connection jump = a fresh breadcrumb rooted at the target** (strip hides in-path channels); **no fetch-all** (page 1 comes free with the channel fetch, then lazy-load); filter `state==available`; **per-section degrade** for dead slugs; **depth cap 8 + cycle guard**. Forks decided: connections = nested + strip (not a full graph); root = several channels; logo/about folded in; Board deferred. Graduates a **self-hosting-config** ticket (config shape + delivery + `info.json` migration). Board stays fog. Does not decide embedding fallbacks (`binder-ab29`) or the framework (`binder-c14b`).
closeReason: Organizing model decided (docs/design/organizing-model.md): site=several top-level channels; per-class renderer in one slot; uniform breadcrumb drill (cap8+cycle guard); hash-routing kept; no fetch-all. Grilled + adversarially hardened. Graduated a config ticket.

title: Decide per-block embedding and the non-iframeable fallback
description: ## Question Decide how each Are.na block type is embedded, and the fallback when a URL refuses framing. Today everything loads in one `
closeReason: Embedding decided (docs/design/embedding.md): per-class renderers; Media=sandboxed srcdoc embed; static app can't detect framing refusal -> shipped hostname denylist + open-in-new-tab escape + fallback card. Empirically header-probed + 2x adversarial critic.

title: Evaluate Are.na API V3 vs staying on deprecated V2
description:
## Question

Evaluate Are.na API **V3** versus staying on the deprecated **V2**. V2 (`api.are.na/v2`) is what Binder targets today — public/no-auth, CORS `*`, empirically verified working — but the V2 docs are banner-marked **deprecated** and point to a V3 at `are.na/developers` whose reference pages are **login-gated** (unverified during the API research).

Determine: V3's base URL and public-channel read endpoints; whether V3 supports **unauthenticated public-channel reads with CORS**; any announced **V2 sunset** timeline; the migration delta. Recommend **V2-now-behind-an-adapter** vs **V3-now**. Use `/research`.

## Answer

Recommendation: **build on V3 now** (reverses the earlier V2-adapter lean). Full writeup: **`docs/research/arena-api-v3.md`**. Empirically probed live + `are.na/developers/explore` (2026-07-11).

- **V3 is live, public-read, CORS `*`, no auth** — verified (`GET /v3/channels/:slug` → 200, `ACAO: *`; reads are "Optional"-auth). It's the actively-documented going-forward REST API; **V2 is deprecated (no sunset date published)** — building a new app on it is a false economy.
- **Migration delta is mechanical, not a redesign:** `class`→`type`, `Media`→`Embed`, `content_html`→`content.html`, `image.display.url`→`image.large`; **`embed.html` / `source.url` / `attachment.url` unchanged**. `generated_title` gone (derive the label); `position` moved into the per-block `connection` object; contents via `{data, meta}` (`has_more_pages`); `visibility` replaces `status`; `counts` replaces `length`. Full table in the doc.
- **New: V3 publishes & enforces rate limits** — guest **30/min**, `X-RateLimit-*` headers, `429`. This constrains *and* helps: it validates the lazy/cached/no-fetch-all decisions and adds a concrete **429-backoff** requirement. 30/min is fine per-user with caching; a token (Free 120) is a power-user toggle (same secret-exposure tradeoff → default guest).

Consequences: standing **Data** decision → V3; `organizing-model.md` + `embedding.md` now carry a V3 banner (field-name translation is a migration task); the runtime-resilience fog gains the `429`/`X-RateLimit` requirement. No new ticket.
closeReason: Build on V3. V3 is live/public/CORS/no-auth (verified); V2 deprecated (no sunset). Delta mechanical (embed.html/source.url/attachment.url unchanged). V3 publishes rate limits (guest 30/min + headers). docs/research/arena-api-v3.md

title: Sketch a wireframe-storyboard of the Binder × Are.na experience
description: ## Question Make the reimagined Binder concrete: a cheap, low-fidelity **wireframe-storyboard** — a sequence of rough screens/states — of the whole Are.na-driven experience, as something to react to *now*, before the deeper design decisions harden. Cover the journey end to end: - **Landing** — first load: a channel resolved into a nav, with title/about drawn from the channel. - **Browsing the nav** — how entries read; how nested channels / connections surface (drill-in? tree? sections?). - **Selecting block types** — the frame/inline state for a Link (iframe), a Media embed, an Image, a Text block, an Attachment (PDF). - **The non-iframeable fallback** — what a Link that refuses framing looks like. - **The signature UX** — whether the draggable movable menu and box-shadow aesthetic survive; mobile. Deliver rough frames (ASCII/box wireframes, an outline, or a stub) as a linked asset via `/prototype`. Scope note: this is the connective, whole-experience sketch — it *feeds* the rigorous per-area tickets *Design the channel-to-navigation organizing model* (`binder-7ac4`) and *Decide per-block embedding and the non-iframeable fallback* (`binder-ab29`); it is not a duplicate of them. Grounded in the verified API facts (`docs/research/arena-public-api.md`). ## Answer Full storyboard (9 ASCII frames): **`docs/design/binder-arena-storyboard.md`**. Explored 3 directions in parallel and judged them; chose **"Same Box, Deeper Stack"** (41/45) as the spine, grafting the best of the other two: - **Spine — preserve both signatures.** The floating **draggable** double-box-shadow monospace menu *and* the **full-viewport background** stay. One `GET /channels/:slug` resolves the whole site (title→header, description→about, blocks→a numbered index). A **per-class renderer** fills the single background slot: Link→iframe `source.url`, Media→injected `embed.html` (embedly, framing-safe), Image→``, Text→`content_html`, Attachment→PDF in-frame. Nested channels **drill in place** with a breadcrumb. - **Grafts:** a **connections band** (channel↔channel cross-links — the "third leg" the block-tree flattens) and an **optional Board** grid for large channels (both from the Are.na-native direction); the calm **numbered index**, an explicit **loading/resilience frame**, and a **`link!`** pre-warning glyph (from the reader-first direction). - **Correctness fix:** active row is **BLACK** (the live `.active` rule), not red. **Two forks handed to `binder-7ac4`:** (1) fold logo/`.about` into the panel vs keep the corner wordmark; (2) offer the opt-in Board view or stay list-only. **Risks carried forward:** framing detection is heuristic (denylist + timeout → `binder-ab29`); injected `embed.html` is a third-party-HTML trust surface (→ `binder-ab29`); deep/cyclic drill needs a depth cap + cycle guard (→ `binder-7ac4`); `generated_title` can be "Untitled"/truncated; runtime dependence on the deprecated V2 API (→ `binder-d4d4`). Does not resolve `binder-7ac4` or `binder-ab29` — it *pre-stages* their decisions with a concrete artifact to react to.
closeReason: Storyboard delivered: docs/design/binder-arena-storyboard.md. Chose 'Same Box, Deeper Stack' (preserve draggable box-shadow menu + full-viewport slot; per-class renderer; breadcrumb drill; connections band; opt-in Board; fallback card). Pre-stages binder-7ac4 & binder-ab29.

title: Design the self-hosting config: site meta, channel list & theming
description:
## Question

Design Binder's self-hosting **config**: how a self-hoster declares their site and themes it. The organizing model settled that a site = an optional title/about + an **ordered list of Are.na channel slugs** (the sections). Decide:

- **Shape & delivery** — a fetched `config.json` (runtime, matches the no-build data path) vs URL params (`?channels=a,b,c`) vs a small build-time config. Given runtime-fetch + static hosting, `config.json` is the leaning default.
- **Theming** — what a self-hoster may restyle: is the signature box-shadow / monospace identity fixed, or exposed as overridable tokens? How?
- **Migration** — this replaces today's `info.json` (`title` / `logo` / `about` + a `menu` of name→URL). Specify the path from that shape to channel-slug sections.

Use `/grilling`.

## Answer

Decided: **`docs/design/config.md`**.

- **Delivery:** a **runtime-fetched `config.json`** next to `index.html` (edit-and-reload, no rebuild) + a `?channel=slug` / `?channels=a,b,c` **URL override** for a zero-config / shareable deploy. Precedence: URL params → `config.json.channels` → a "configure me" empty state (never a crash).
- **Schema:** `{ title?, about?, logo?, channels[] (required — the sections), theme? }`. `channels` accepts slugs or full are.na URLs. **No auth/token field** (consistent with public-only / no-secrets).
- **Theming:** the **signature look is the default, fully tokenized** (your pick). CSS custom properties (`--binder-panel-bg`, `--binder-shadow-1/2`, `--binder-border`, `--binder-font`, `--binder-text`, `--binder-accent`) live in a **global** stylesheet (Svelte scoped styles don't reach `{@html}`), defaulting to the classic box-shadow/monospace; `config.theme` overrides any subset for a full restyle.
- **Migration:** `config.json` supersedes `info.json`; `title`/`about`/`logo` carry over; `menu` (name→URL) → `channels` (slugs) is a **content-model shift** — the self-hoster puts content into an Are.na channel (old URLs → Link blocks) and lists the slug(s). Documented; no auto-converter.

No new ticket. Feeds the codebase-migration fog (config loading + the `info.json`→`config.json` cutover).
closeReason: Config decided (docs/design/config.md): runtime config.json + ?channel override; schema {title,about,logo,channels[],theme}; signature look default + tokenized via CSS vars; info.json->config.json content-model migration.

title: Name the rebuilt tool — keep "Binder" or rename?
description:
## Question

Should the modernized, Are.na-backed rebuild keep the name **"Binder"** or take a **new name**? If renaming: brainstorm candidates fitting the binding/collecting/connecting metaphor + an Are.na-native feel; short, memorable; with an available domain + GitHub repo. Consider continuity (the `clementvalla/binder` repo, the binder-clip metaphor) vs a fresh identity.

## Answer

Chosen: **AreNotebook** (Are.na + notebook). Detail: **`docs/design/name.md`**.

- **Availability verified fully clear** (2026-07-11): npm `arenotebook` free, `github.com/arenotebook` free, `arenotebook.com` + `arenotebook.io` both unregistered.
- **Flagged caveat (not blocking, trivially reversible):** the name leans on the Are.na brand (association / trademark risk) and parses a little awkwardly spoken — worth a "for Are.na" framing or a quick trademark sanity-check before the rebrand propagates.
- **Considered:** keep Binder (continuity default); Sheaf / Weft / Vitrine / Tessle — from a 32-candidate brainstorm across four lenses, availability-vetted.

Rebrand (repo / docs / `binder-clip` assets) rides with the codebase migration.
closeReason: Name: AreNotebook (Are.na + notebook). Availability fully clear (npm+gh-org+.com/.io free). Caveat flagged (brand-association/trademark, reversible). docs/design/name.md

title: AreNotebook: health & surplus (tend 2026-07-11)
description:
## Notes

Domain: AreNotebook — a 100% static Svelte 5 + Vite app that turns Are.na public channels into a navigable site. This map tracks the **health & surplus** backlog from the tend run of 2026-07-11 (commit ae5c299, branch arenotebook-build).

Detail store: **ISSUES.md** at repo root holds the full Evidence · Rungs · Lesson · runnable Loop · Strength for every ticket. Each ticket here gists and points at its I#/D# there — the loop in ISSUES.md is paste-ready and standalone.

Skills each session consults: the tend LOOPS.md patterns (canonicalization, claim-vs-reality, silence audit, coverage climb, journey walk, negative-space, deletion sweep, dark-data census, headroom audit) — already instantiated per ticket in ISSUES.md. Directions (D*) are classify-only: they produce a classified ledger and STOP, no build.

Standing preferences: no AI attribution/co-author trailers on commits; one fix per commit, message references the ledger row; ledgers live under ledgers/ and are kept. **Gate:** I1 is a live security finding — no growth (direction verdicts / graft) converges while I1 is open.

## Decisions so far

<!-- one line per closed ticket -->

## Fog

- **D5 Board view** (per-channel thumbnail grid) — handed to graft, not a ticket here: it would have to be built, not tended. Cover.svelte + covers.js are the primitive to build on.
- Dropped from the tend walk (not tickets): FallbackCard over-generality (reached via real paths, classifies healthy); the stray console.log in the new textblock test (trivial, author owns the uncommitted batch).
- I6 (home Cover off the boot journey) needs the author's intent on whether boot should land on the Cover or auto-enter — a decision point inside that ticket, not yet its own ticket. sinks inherit it. Gate lifted: directions unblocked.} sinks inherit it. Gate lifted: directions unblocked./- [Sanitize remote channel-description HTML](binder-b680) — sanitize at the Nav.about getter; both {@html} sinks inherit it. Gate lifted: directions unblocked.
- [Cache Are.na connections fetches](binder-be2a) — connCache in arena.js; strip no longer re-fetched per nav.
- [Single-source DEPTH_CAP](binder-726d) — nav imports it from router.js; locked in CLAUDE.md.
- [Surface connection-fetch failures](binder-265a) — #loadConnections + config load now warn; parse-edges stay silent by design.
- [Reconcile config.md theme tokens](binder-076c) — --binder-* -> --an-* + historical banner; docs are decision records.
- [Document ?channel= config limitation](binder-d725) — README states params mode is channels-only.
- [Deletion sweep](binder-7261) — removed 3 dead files + onHash/denylist() exports; model.js kept whole (ACL).
- [Test drag.js + renderers](binder-c07b) — drag state machine (6) + AttachmentBlock fork (5) + LinkBlock hatch (1).
- [Hybrid boot landing](binder-1116) — 1 section auto-enters, 2+ land on the home Cover as a section index.
- [Directions census](ledgers/DARKDATA.md) — connectionMode/blurhash/aspectRatio/embedType classified dark; per/nextPage pinned (ledgers/HEADROOM.md). Classify-only; verdicts deferred.
- [Test drag.js + renderers](binder-c07b) — drag state machine (6) + AttachmentBlock fork (5) + LinkBlock hatch (1).
- [Hybrid boot landing](binder-1116) — 1 section auto-enters, 2+ land on the home Cover as a section index.
- [Directions census](ledgers/DARKDATA.md) — connectionMode/blurhash/aspectRatio/embedType classified dark; per/nextPage pinned (ledgers/HEADROOM.md). Classify-only; verdicts deferred.
- [Test drag.js + renderers](binder-c07b) — drag state machine (6) + AttachmentBlock fork (5) + LinkBlock hatch (1).
- [Hybrid boot landing](binder-1116) — 1 section auto-enters, 2+ land on the home Cover as a section index.

title: Sanitize remote channel-description HTML before {@html}
description:
## Question
Remote Are.na channel-description HTML reaches {@html} raw at Panel.svelte:46 and Cover.svelte:16 (via nav.about), bypassing sanitize.js — a live XSS surface. Canonicalize every {@html} sink onto one sanitized path.

Rungs: L4<->L2 (security) | Strength: Strong | Gate: closes the platform check; blocks all direction verdicts.
Loop & full evidence: ISSUES.md#I1 (instantiated canonicalization loop; ledger ledgers/CANON.md; lock rule to CLAUDE.md).

## Answer
Sanitized at the source: the Nav.about getter (src/lib/nav.svelte.js) routes remote channel `description` and config `about` through sanitizeHtml. Both {@html} sinks — Panel.svelte:46 and Cover.svelte:16 — are fed `nav.about` via App.svelte, so neither can receive raw remote HTML; one canonical sanitized path, not two sinks each remembering. Test: nav.test.js asserts a <script>/onerror payload in a channel description is stripped. Lock: CLAUDE.md security invariant (all remote HTML through sanitizeHtml before {@html}). Fix commit follows.
closeReason: sanitize remote about-HTML at Nav.about; test + CLAUDE.md lock

title: Cache Are.na connections fetches
description:
## Question
getConnections (arena.js:79) has no cache while metaCache/pageCache exist; nav re-fetches on every enter/pop/jump, against the README in-session caching promise. Add a connectionsCache or reconcile the docs.

Rungs: L3<->L4 | Strength: Strong.
Loop & full evidence: ISSUES.md#I2 (claim-vs-reality diff; ledger ledgers/CLAIMS.md).

## Answer
Added connCache (SvelteMap, slug -> {channels}) to createArena, mirroring metaCache/pageCache; getConnections returns the cached result on repeat. Re-drilling/popping/jumping to a channel no longer re-hits /connections. Test asserts the second call does not refetch. This also makes the README rate-limits claim ("caching channels in-session") accurate for connections — no doc change needed.

## Answer
Added connCache (SvelteMap, slug -> {channels}) to createArena, mirroring metaCache/pageCache; getConnections returns the cached result on repeat. Re-drilling/popping/jumping to a channel no longer re-hits /connections. Test asserts the second call does not refetch. This also makes the README rate-limits claim ("caching channels in-session") accurate for connections — no doc change needed.

## Answer
Added connCache (SvelteMap, slug -> {channels}) to createArena, mirroring metaCache/pageCache; getConnections returns the cached result on repeat. Re-drilling/popping/jumping to a channel no longer re-hits /connections. Test asserts the second call does not refetch. This also makes the README rate-limits claim ("caching channels in-session") accurate for connections — no doc change needed.
closeReason: connCache in arena.js; connections cached in-session; test added

title: Reconcile design docs: Binder/V2 -> AreNotebook/V3
description:
## Question
docs/design/{config,organizing-model,embedding}.md document --binder-* tokens and V2 fields; code uses --an-* and V3. Diff every doc claim vs code; fix or banner each.

Rungs: L1<->L4 | Strength: Worth exploring.
Loop & full evidence: ISSUES.md#I3 (claim-vs-reality diff; ledger ledgers/CLAIMS.md).

## Answer
The three design docs are build-time DECISION RECORDS (each "Resolves binder-XXXX (decided)"), not current specs — not re-litigated. embedding.md and organizing-model.md already carry V2->V3 translation banners at the top (self-labelled historical). config.md lacked one and additionally documented --binder-* CSS vars that never shipped (code finalized on --an-*, src/lib/theme.js; the config theme *keys* are unprefixed and were already correct). Fixed: corrected the token table --binder-* -> --an-*, added a status banner pointing to README as the live guide. Verified no --binder- var ref remains anywhere but the banner that explains the correction. README is already accurate (checked in the tend walk).

Verified no --binder- var ref remains anywhere but the banner that explains the correction. README is already accurate (checked in the tend walk).

Verified no --binder- var ref remains anywhere but the banner that explains the correction. README is already accurate (checked in the tend walk).

title: Surface connection-fetch failures (silence audit)
description:
## Question
nav.svelte.js:98-100 swallows connection-fetch failures into connections=[], indistinguishable from an empty set or a 429. Census every handler; surface network/API failures.

Rungs: L4<->L2 | Strength: Worth exploring.
Loop & full evidence: ISSUES.md#I4 (silence audit; ledger ledgers/SILENCE.md).

## Answer
Silence audit of src/ catch sites. Two swallowed a *network* failure and are now surfaced with console.warn: #loadConnections (nav.svelte.js) — a failed/429 connections fetch is warned (with a rate-limited note) yet still degrades to a calm empty strip, so it is no longer indistinguishable from a channel with no connections; and config.js config.json load — warned so a malformed config isn't silently swallowed into the empty state. The channel-load path (#loadChannel) already surfaced rate-limited vs unreachable to the user (pre-existing). Parse-edge catches (model.js:34/51, denylist.js:24, drag.js:49) legitimately stay silent — safe defaults, not swallowed failures. Test forces a rate-limited connections throw and asserts the warn fires + empty degradation.

## Answer

title: Cover drag.js and the renderer layer with tests
description:
## Question
drag.js (66-line pointer state machine) and the individual renderers have zero test cover — the scariest files to change. Climb coverage on core interaction logic.

Rungs: L4<->L3 | Strength: Worth exploring.
Loop & full evidence: ISSUES.md#I5 (coverage climb; ledger ledgers/COVERAGE.md; threshold 70%).

## Answer
Netted the risk-bearing interaction/renderer logic that had zero cover:
- drag.js (6 tests): pin+move by pointer delta, controls-are-not-drags guard, mobile (<=768px) disable, foreign pointerId ignored, release on pointerup, destroy() cleanup. This was the flagged highest-risk file (stateful pointer state machine, no net).
- AttachmentBlock (5 tests): the contentType x device fork — desktop PDF inlines, mobile PDF degrades to a card, video/audio native, unhandled -> FallbackCard. flushSync needed since the mobile decision rides a $effect+matchMedia.
- LinkBlock (1 test): the always-present "open in new tab" escape hatch + framed src (its old 2s prominence timer was removed in the link-framing fix, nothing stateful left).
Deferred (low risk, purely presentational): ImageBlock, EmbedBlock, FallbackCard. No bug exposed; the mobile-PDF case validated the fork. Suite 87 green.

## Answer

- AttachmentBlock (5 tests): the contentType x device fork — desktop PDF inlines, mobile PDF degrades to a card, video/audio native, unhandled -> FallbackCard. flushSync needed since the mobile decision rides a $effect+matchMedia.
- LinkBlock (1 test): the always-present "open in new tab" escape hatch + framed src (its old 2s prominence timer was removed in the link-framing fix, nothing stateful left).
Deferred (low risk, purely presentational): ImageBlock, EmbedBlock, FallbackCard. No bug exposed; the mobile-PDF case validated the fork. Suite 87 green.

## Answer
Netted the risk-bearing interaction/renderer logic that had zero cover:
- drag.js (6 tests): pin+move by pointer delta, controls-are-not-drags guard, mobile (<=768px) disable, foreign pointerId ignored, release on pointerup, destroy() cleanup. This was the flagged highest-risk file (stateful pointer state machine, no net).
- AttachmentBlock (5 tests): the contentType x device fork — desktop PDF inlines, mobile PDF degrades to a card, video/audio native, unhandled -> FallbackCard. flushSync needed since the mobile decision rides a $effect+matchMedia.
- LinkBlock (1 test): the always-present "open in new tab" escape hatch + framed src (its old 2s prominence timer was removed in the link-framing fix, nothing stateful left).
Deferred (low risk, purely presentational): ImageBlock, EmbedBlock, FallbackCard. No bug exposed; the mobile-PDF case validated the fork. Suite 87 green.
closeReason: drag.js + AttachmentBlock fork + LinkBlock hatch tested (12 tests); presentational renderers deferred

title: Put the home Cover on the boot journey
description:
## Question
App.svelte auto-enters the first section on empty hash, so nav.atRoot is false and the new Cover (gated on atRoot) never shows on first load though collectThumbnails runs every boot. Journey-walk it; decide with author whether boot lands on Cover or auto-enters.

Rungs: L2<->L2 seam | Strength: Worth exploring | Note: live uncommitted work; author decision inside.
Loop & full evidence: ISSUES.md#I6 (journey walk; ledger ledgers/JOURNEY.md).

## Answer
Resolved as HYBRID (author decision): on an empty hash, a single section auto-enters (no point covering one item) while 2+ sections stay at root so the home Cover shows as a section index; deep-link hashes are always honored. Changed App.svelte auto-enter guard from `nav.sections[0] && !dead` to `nav.sections.length === 1 && !dead`. The Cover (built but off the main path — it only appeared after navigating back to root) is now the front door for multi-section sites. collectThumbnails still warms the cache every boot (beneficial either branch). Tests: existing single-section smoke test still auto-enters; new smoke test asserts 2 sections -> Cover with hash staying empty. Suite 88 green, build clean.

## Answer
Resolved as HYBRID (author decision): on an empty hash, a single section auto-enters (no point covering one item) while 2+ sections stay at root so the home Cover shows as a section index; deep-link hashes are always honored. Changed App.svelte auto-enter guard from `nav.sections[0] && !dead` to `nav.sections.length === 1 && !dead`. The Cover (built but off the main path — it only appeared after navigating back to root) is now the front door for multi-section sites. collectThumbnails still warms the cache every boot (beneficial either branch). Tests: existing single-section smoke test still auto-enters; new smoke test asserts 2 sections -> Cover with hash staying empty. Suite 88 green, build clean.

## Answer
Resolved as HYBRID (author decision): on an empty hash, a single section auto-enters (no point covering one item) while 2+ sections stay at root so the home Cover shows as a section index; deep-link hashes are always honored. Changed App.svelte auto-enter guard from `nav.sections[0] && !dead` to `nav.sections.length === 1 && !dead`. The Cover (built but off the main path — it only appeared after navigating back to root) is now the front door for multi-section sites. collectThumbnails still warms the cache every boot (beneficial either branch). Tests: existing single-section smoke test still auto-enters; new smoke test asserts 2 sections -> Cover with hash staying empty. Suite 88 green, build clean.
closeReason: hybrid boot: 1 section auto-enters, 2+ land on the Cover; deep-links honored; tested

title: Close the ?channel= vs config.json feature gap
description:
## Question
config.js params branch returns only {channels}; ?channel= mode silently drops title/about/logo/theme vs config.json. Either accept ?title=/?theme= params or document the limitation.

Rungs: L2 seam | Strength: Worth exploring.
Loop & full evidence: ISSUES.md#I7 (negative-space matrix; ledger ledgers/NEGSPACE.md).

## Answer
Resolved as "document the limitation" (the loop's other branch). Per config.md, params mode is intentionally the zero-config shareable path; a half-set of ?title=/?about= params would be a worse half-feature and ?theme= (an object) doesn't belong in a URL. README zero-config section now states params mode sets only channels — title/about/logo/theme come from config.json, default look otherwise. Added a code comment at the params return (config.js) so it reads as intentional, not a bug. No behavior change.

## Answer

README zero-config section now states params mode sets only channels — title/about/logo/theme come from config.json, default look otherwise. Added a code comment at the params return (config.js) so it reads as intentional, not a bug. No behavior change.

## Answer

title: Single-source the DEPTH_CAP invariant
description:
## Question
DEPTH_CAP is declared in both router.js:7 and nav.svelte.js:13 — one invariant, two sources that can drift. Export one constant, import in the other.

Rungs: L3 | Strength: Worth exploring.
Loop & full evidence: ISSUES.md#I8 (canonicalization; ledger ledgers/CANON.md).

## Answer
router.js already exported DEPTH_CAP; nav.svelte.js was redeclaring its own const. Removed the redeclaration and imported DEPTH_CAP from router.js — one source of truth for the drill-depth / hash-encoding cap. CLAUDE.md locks the invariant. Existing depth-cap test (nav.test.js) still passes (value unchanged at 8).

title: Deletion sweep: dead files, orphaned exports, model.js god-module
description:
## Question
Inert legacy root files (style.css, readme.txt, todo.txt), orphaned exports (onHash, denylist(), ARENA_BASE), and the model.js god-module. Classify dead/healthy/god; delete dead, split-plan the god.

Rungs: L3 | Strength: Worth exploring.
Loop & full evidence: ISSUES.md#I9 (deletion sweep; ledger ledgers/DEADWOOD.md).

## Answer
Classified by inbound-reference trace, then removed the dead:
- DELETED (zero refs): style.css, readme.txt, todo.txt — pre-rebuild Binder artifacts; index.html/main.js load src/styles/global.css.
- REMOVED orphaned exports: router.js onHash() (no importer; App wires hashchange directly), denylist.js denylist() array export (only isDenylisted is consumed).
- UN-EXPORTED arena.js ARENA_BASE (no external importer; now a module const).
- model.js GOD-MODULE VERDICT: stays whole. Its wide fan-in is the correct shape of a normalization / anti-corruption layer — every renderer and nav node SHOULD depend on one NormBlock model; that is cohesion, not a god-module smell (no unrelated responsibilities glued together). Optional future seam if URL logic grows: extract extractSlug + isArenaChannelLink (+ ARENA_HOST_RE) into src/lib/arena-url.js, leaving model.js as pure normalization. Not executed.
Full suite green (75) and vite build succeeds after removals.

- UN-EXPORTED arena.js ARENA_BASE (no external importer; now a module const).
- model.js GOD-MODULE VERDICT: stays whole. Its wide fan-in is the correct shape of a normalization / anti-corruption layer — every renderer and nav node SHOULD depend on one NormBlock model; that is cohesion, not a god-module smell (no unrelated responsibilities glued together). Optional future seam if URL logic grows: extract extractSlug + isArenaChannelLink (+ ARENA_HOST_RE) into src/lib/arena-url.js, leaving model.js as pure normalization. Not executed.
Full suite green (75) and vite build succeeds after removals.

- model.js GOD-MODULE VERDICT: stays whole. Optional future seam if URL logic grows: extract extractSlug + isArenaChannelLink (+ ARENA_HOST_RE) into src/lib/arena-url.js, leaving model.js as pure normalization. Not executed.
Full suite green (75) and vite build succeeds after removals.

description:

title: Headroom: per / nextPage pinned pagination
description:
## Question
getContentsPage per defaults to 100, no caller varies it; nextPage is computed into cache but never read. Classify pinned/dark; read intent. Build/unpin nothing.

Rungs: L4->L3 | Strength: Speculative | Blocked by I1 (gate).
Loop & full evidence: ISSUES.md#D4 (headroom audit; ledger ledgers/HEADROOM.md).

## Answer
per: PINNED to 100 at every getContentsPage caller (none pass it). nextPage: computed at arena.js:74, never read (nav rolls its own #page). Intent: per = convenience default matching Are.na max page (unknown/no ADR); nextPage = forgotten-latent server cursor.
Classified ledger: ledgers/HEADROOM.md. Classify-only — verdict/commissioned-as left empty for a later pass.
